name: Phishing Campaigns
description: Sophisticated social engineering attacks targeting credentials through deceptive emails and fake login pages.
mitigation: MFA, email filters, user training, DMARC/DKIM/SPF

name: Ransomware
description: Malware that encrypts files and demands payment. Ransomware-as-a-Service has lowered the barrier to entry.
mitigation: Offline backups, EDR, patch management, disable macros

name: Zero-Day Exploits
description: Previously unknown vulnerabilities exploited before patches are available. Increasingly traded on dark web markets.
mitigation: Virtual patching, threat intelligence, application whitelisting

name: DDoS Attacks
description: Distributed denial-of-service attacks overwhelming network resources. Amplification attacks on the rise.
mitigation: CDN/DDoS protection, rate limiting, traffic scrubbing

name: Social Engineering
description: Psychological manipulation to trick users into divulging confidential information or performing actions.

name: Insider Threats
description: Security risks originating from within the organization — employees, contractors, or partners.
mitigation: UEBA, least privilege, access reviews, DLP

name: Credential Stuffing
description: Automated login attempts using leaked username and password pairs from previous breaches.
mitigation: MFA, rate limiting, bot detection, password reuse checks

name: Supply Chain Attacks
description: Compromise of vendors, libraries, updates, or third-party tools to reach downstream targets.
mitigation: SBOM review, signed builds, vendor risk checks, dependency pinning

name: Business Email Compromise
description: Fraud campaigns that impersonate executives, vendors, or finance staff to redirect payments or data.
mitigation: Payment verification, DMARC, approval workflows, awareness training

name: Cloud Misconfiguration
description: Exposed storage, excessive IAM permissions, or weak logging that can leak data or enable compromise.
mitigation: CSPM checks, least privilege IAM, encryption, continuous audit logging

name: Malvertising
description: Malicious advertisements or compromised ad networks that redirect users to scams or exploit kits.
mitigation: Browser protection, ad filtering, patching, DNS security controls
